Pagina User Guide

A CSS Paged Media to PDF Engine Written in Rust

Chapter 1: Introduction

Pagina is a high-performance HTML and CSS to PDF conversion engine built entirely in Rust. It leverages battle-tested components from the Servo browser engine project for HTML and CSS parsing, combined with a custom paged layout engine that implements the CSS Paged Media specification.

The goal of Pagina is to provide Prince-level CSS Paged Media support as an open-source tool, enabling developers to generate professional-quality PDF documents from HTML and CSS templates.

Key features include page sizing and margins, running headers and footers with page numbers, footnotesFootnotes are placed at the bottom of the page where they are referenced, following the CSS Generated Content for Paged Media specification., page break control, and a growing set of CSS property support.

Why Pagina?

Existing open-source tools for HTML-to-PDF conversion each have significant limitations. WeasyPrint lacks footnote support. Paged.js depends on a browser engine. Vivliostyle uses the AGPL license. Pagina aims to fill this gap with a fast, permissively-licensed, standalone tool.

Architecture

The conversion pipeline consists of four stages: HTML parsing with html5ever, CSS parsing with cssparser, styled tree construction with cascade resolution, and paged layout with PDF output via printpdf.

Chapter 2: CSS Paged Media Features

Pagina implements the core features of the CSS Paged Media Module Level 3 and CSS Generated Content for Paged Media (GCPM) specifications.

Page Size and Margins

The page size is set using the size property in an at-page rule. Named sizes like A4, A3, Letter, and Legal are supported, along with explicit dimensions in any CSS length unit. Orientation can be specified with portrait or landscape keywords.

Margin Boxes

Up to 16 margin boxes can be placed in the page margins. The most commonly used positions are top-left, top-center, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-center, and bottom-right. Content can include static text, page counters, and running strings.

Running Headers

The string-set property captures element content into a named string. This string can then be referenced in margin boxes using the string() function, creating running headers that automatically update as the document flows across pages.

Footnotes

Elements with float: footnote are removed from the normal flow and placed at the bottom of the page where they are referenced. A footnote reference marker is inserted at the point of reference, and the footnote text appears with a matching marker at the page bottomThis is a second footnote demonstrating that multiple footnotes can appear on the same page..

Page Breaks

The break-before and break-after properties control where page breaks occur. Setting break-before: page on a heading ensures each chapter starts on a new page. The break-inside: avoid property prevents breaks within an element.

Chapter 3: Supported CSS Properties

Pagina supports a growing set of CSS properties for document formatting.

Typography

  • font-size: Any CSS length unit (pt, mm, px, em, etc.)
  • font-weight: normal, bold
  • font-style: normal, italic
  • font-family: Helvetica (sans-serif) and Courier (monospace)
  • line-height: Unitless ratio or length
  • text-align: left, center, right, justify
  • color: Named colors, hex codes, rgb()

Box Model

  • margin: Shorthand and individual sides (top, right, bottom, left)
  • padding: Top and bottom padding
  • border-bottom: Width, style, and color

Layout

  • display: block, inline, none, list-item
  • break-before / break-after: page, avoid, auto
  • float: footnote (CSS GCPM)
  • string-set: Named string capture (CSS GCPM)

Chapter 4: Usage

Command Line

The simplest way to use Pagina is through the command-line interface:

pagina input.html -o output.pdf

As a Library

Pagina can also be used as a Rust library for programmatic PDF generation:

let pdf_bytes = pagina_core::convert(&html_string);

Comparison with Prince

FeaturePrincePagina
CSS Paged MediaFullCore features
Margin boxesAll 16All 16
FootnotesYesYes
Running headersYesYes
LicenseProprietary ($495+)MIT / Apache-2.0
LanguageC/C++Rust

Note: Pagina is under active development. Feature parity with Prince is a long-term goal.
